zotero.abstractNote: The notion of compressibility is central to compressed sensing and high-dimensional statistics. While the compressibility of a vector is often defined via the decay rate of its sorted coefficients, this definition is not always suitable for random vectors, especially when the underlying distribution is not supported on a coordinate-aligned set. In this paper, we introduce a new framework for the compressibility of random vectors based on the notion of affine singularity. A random vector is said to be affinely singular if it is supported on a low-dimensional affine subspace. We propose a measure of compressibility for such vectors, which we call the affine compressibility measure, and show that it captures the essential features of compressibility in a coordinate-independent manner. We further demonstrate the utility of this measure by providing new bounds on the performance of compressed sensing recovery algorithms for affinely singular signals.

extra.Conference Name: 2020 IEEE International Symposium on Information Theory (ISIT)
